projectDescription: An open-source, self-hosted AI platform for enterprises that gives you an OpenAI-compatible API, management dashboard, multi-node orchestration, and enterprise auth — with zero data egress.
keyFeatures: OpenAI-compatible API — drop-in replacement for existing SDK code, just point base_url at your instance
Multi-model and multi-GPU orchestration with automatic load balancing and least-connections routing
Web dashboard with RBAC — five roles (owner, admin, member, labeler, viewer) and full audit trail
Enterprise auth — SSO/SAML, 2FA/TOTP, passkeys (WebAuthn), and multi-tenant organization isolation
Model deployment lifecycle management — pull, install, monitor, and canary-deploy LLMs from the dashboard
Fine-tuning and distillation pipeline for custom model creation
MCP server — let AI assistants manage your infrastructure via natural language
Three deployment methods: Xinity CLI (systemd), Docker Compose, and NixOS flake modules
Usage tracking, call logging, and data labeling for every inference request
Inference engine agnostic — uses Ollama and vLLM under the hood, switchable per model
Zero data egress — all processing stays on your own hardware
publicApiSurface: POST /v1/chat/completions — OpenAI-compatible chat endpoint (streaming and non-streaming)
POST /v1/responses — Responses API with persistent store
GET/POST /api/[...rest] — Dashboard management REST API (OpenAPI schema at /api/openapi.json)
GET /mcp — Model Context Protocol endpoint for AI assistant integrations
xinity up <component> — Install/update gateway, dashboard, daemon, db, or all
xinity act <route> [data] — Call any dashboard API route from the CLI
xinity configure <component> — Reconfigure a service interactively
xinity update — Update CLI and installed components
GET /api/v1/models — Infoserver: list all models in the catalog
GET /api/v1/models/:specifier — Infoserver: look up a specific model
